earningCriteria.title: Complete the Remote Work Professional Certification (Self-study or workshop) Achieve a mastery score of 60% or better on the assessment.

items.title: PENTESTERLAB: I consider PentesterLab to be a great resource for learning about web application security and ways how it can be subverted. items.title: Fast_Fourier_Transformation: The FFT is an optimized algorithm for the implementation of the "Discrete Fourier Transformation" (DFT). A signal is sampled over a period of time and divided into its frequency components. These components are single sinusoidal oscillations at distinct frequencies each with their own amplitude and phase. This transformation is illustrated in the following diagram. Over the time period measured, the signal contains 3 distinct dominant frequencies.

payload.name: Fast_Fourier_Transformation

This credential earner understands mindfulness concepts and techniques, and has learned how to apply mindfulness practices for various situations. The individual understands how to further develop focus and self-awareness. The earner can use these skills as a foundation for further study in mindfulness and to apply mental and emotional management in any career path they choose.

earningCriteria.title: Pass the end-of-course assessment with a score of 80% or higher.
High school students and educators can complete the credential here.
Adult learners (age 18+) can complete the credential here.
This credential is available to registered users of IBM SkillsBuild:

Complete the Explorations into Mindfulness course, created by IBM and the University of Oxford Mindfulness Centre, which includes lessons, videos, reading assignments, reflection moments, knowledge checks, and opportunities to practice mindfulness techniques.

payload.description: This credential earner demonstrates a foundational understanding of cybersecurity concepts, objectives, and practices. This includes cyber threat groups, types of attacks, social engineering, case studies, overall security strategies, cryptography, and common approaches that organizations take to prevent, detect, and respond to cyber attacks. This also includes an awareness of the job market. Earners can use this knowledge to pursue further education for a variety of roles in cybersecurity.

earningCriteria.title: Pass the end-of-course assessment with a score of 80% or higher.

items.title: Enterprise Design Thinking - Team Essentials for AI

payload.description: This badge earner has demonstrated proficiency in using Enterprise Design Thinking concepts and activities to design responsible artificial intelligence systems with intention and a focus on people.

earningCriteria.title: Note:  sign-in is required to access the course link.

payload.description: The earner has acquired knowledge of applying Enterprise Design Thinking and its value. As a Practitioner, the badge earner finds opportunities to try it out in their every day work.

earningCriteria.title: EARN: Complete the online Enterprise Design Thinking Lessons, or IBM Approved course equivalent. You may also earn this through intimate in-person training and assessment. Contact IBM to learn more at designthinking@us.ibm.com.
Note: The new badge for this one can be found here. However, if you have earned this older badge, you do not need to earn the replacement badge since the content is the same.
Able to discuss and apply basic Enterprise Design Thinking concepts.
Able to identify opportunities to apply Enterprise Design Thinking.
